egypt.question: What river was essential to ancient Egyptian civilization?
egypt.explanation: The Nile River flooded every year, leaving rich soil that let Egyptians grow crops in the middle of a desert!

egypt.question: How long was the Great Pyramid the tallest building in the world?
egypt.explanation: The Great Pyramid held the record for 3,800 years! Nothing taller was built until Lincoln Cathedral in England in 1311.

egypt.question: What material did ancient Egyptians use to write on?
egypt.explanation: Papyrus was made from river reeds and was one of the earliest writing surfaces. Our word 'paper' actually comes from 'papyrus'!

egypt.question: Which animal was sacred in ancient Egypt?
egypt.explanation: Cats were so important in ancient Egypt that hurting one was a serious crime. They were believed to bring good luck!

egypt.question: Who was the last pharaoh of ancient Egypt?
egypt.explanation: Cleopatra VII was the last active ruler of ancient Egypt. Fun fact: she lived closer to our time than to when the pyramids were built!

greece.question: What form of government did ancient Greece invent?
greece.explanation: Democracy means 'rule by the people' in Greek. Athens let citizens vote on laws directly — the first time this happened in history!

greece.question: When were the first Olympic Games held?
greece.explanation: The first Olympics were in 776 BC in Olympia, Greece. Athletes competed naked and the only prize was an olive wreath!

greece.question: What did Alexander the Great accomplish by age 30?
options: Conquered most of the known world
greece.explanation: Alexander the Great created one of the largest empires in history, stretching from Greece to India, all before turning 30!

greece.question: What does the word 'dinosaur' mean in Greek?
greece.explanation: Dinosaur comes from the Greek words 'deinos' (terrible) and 'sauros' (lizard). Many English words come from Greek!

greece.question: How many islands does Greece have?
greece.explanation: Greece has more than 6,000 islands! But only about 227 of them have people living on them.

china.question: How long is the Great Wall of China?
china.explanation: The Great Wall stretches over 13,000 miles — that's more than halfway around the entire Earth!

china.question: Which of these did China NOT invent?
china.explanation: The telescope was invented in the Netherlands by Hans Lippershey. But China did invent paper, gunpowder, printing, and the compass!

china.question: How many clay soldiers are in the Terracotta Army?
china.explanation: Over 8,000 life-sized clay soldiers were buried with Emperor Qin Shi Huang to protect him in the afterlife. Each face is unique!

china.question: Where were fortune cookies actually invented?
china.explanation: Surprise! Fortune cookies were invented in California, not China. Most people in China have never seen one!

china.question: How long does Chinese New Year last?
china.explanation: Chinese New Year celebrations last 15 days, ending with the Lantern Festival. It's the biggest holiday in China!

india.question: What important math concept did India invent?
india.explanation: India invented the number zero! Without it, we couldn't do modern math or build computers. It changed everything!

india.question: How long did it take to build the Taj Mahal?
india.explanation: The Taj Mahal took 22 years and 20,000 workers to build. It was created by Emperor Shah Jahan as a memorial for his wife.

india.question: What board game was invented in India?
india.explanation: Chess was invented in India around 1,500 years ago! It was originally called 'Chaturanga' and was used to practice battle strategy.

india.question: What is India's movie industry called?
india.explanation: Bollywood (based in Mumbai, formerly Bombay) makes over 1,500 films per year — more than Hollywood!

india.question: How many official languages does India have?
india.explanation: India has 22 officially recognized languages! Hindi and English are the most widely used, but hundreds more are spoken across the country.

japan.question: What code of honor did samurai follow?
japan.explanation: Bushido means 'the way of the warrior.' Samurai followed this strict code of loyalty, honor, and discipline for over 700 years.

japan.question: How long was Japan isolated from the world?
japan.explanation: From 1603 to 1868, Japan closed its borders to almost all foreigners. This period is called 'Sakoku' (closed country).

japan.question: What was Tokyo originally called?
japan.explanation: Tokyo was a small fishing village called Edo before becoming the capital. 'Tokyo' means 'Eastern Capital' in Japanese.

japan.question: How many vending machines does Japan have?
japan.explanation: Japan has about 5.5 million vending machines — one for every 23 people! They sell everything from drinks to umbrellas.

japan.question: What is special about the island of Okunoshima?
japan.explanation: Okunoshima is called 'Rabbit Island' because hundreds of friendly wild rabbits live there and run up to visitors!

usa.question: When was the Declaration of Independence signed?
usa.explanation: The Declaration of Independence was signed on July 4, 1776, which is why Americans celebrate the 4th of July!

usa.question: What was the Apollo 11 mission famous for?
usa.explanation: In 1969, Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin became the first humans to walk on the Moon. Armstrong said: 'That's one small step for man, one giant leap for mankind.'

usa.question: Which country gave the Statue of Liberty to the USA?
usa.explanation: France gave the Statue of Liberty to the USA in 1886 as a symbol of friendship and freedom!

usa.question: Which is the largest US state?
usa.explanation: Alaska is massive — it's bigger than Texas, California, and Montana combined! But it has fewer people than most US cities.

usa.question: What has more locations in the US — McDonald's or public libraries?
usa.explanation: There are about 17,000 public libraries in the US compared to about 13,000 McDonald's. Books beat burgers!

brazil.question: What language do people speak in Brazil?
brazil.explanation: Brazil speaks Portuguese because it was colonized by Portugal. It's the only Portuguese-speaking country in South America!

brazil.question: How many times has Brazil won the FIFA World Cup?
brazil.explanation: Brazil has won the World Cup 5 times (1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, 2002) — more than any other country!

brazil.question: What percentage of the world's oxygen does the Amazon Rainforest produce?
brazil.explanation: The Amazon produces about 20% of the world's oxygen, which is why it's called 'the lungs of the Earth.'

brazil.question: What color are Amazon river dolphins?
brazil.explanation: Amazon river dolphins (called boto) are actually pink! Scientists think it might be from blood vessels close to their skin.

brazil.question: How quickly was Brazil's capital Brasilia built?
brazil.explanation: Brasilia was built from scratch in just 41 months in the 1960s. The whole city was planned and constructed at incredible speed!

france.question: Why was the Eiffel Tower originally built?
france.explanation: The Eiffel Tower was built as a temporary entrance for the 1889 World's Fair. People loved it so much they kept it!

france.question: What happened during the French Revolution?
france.explanation: In 1789, the French people overthrew King Louis XVI. The revolution's ideas of liberty and equality inspired people around the world.

france.question: How many tourists visit France each year?
france.explanation: About 90 million tourists visit France each year, making it the most visited country in the world!

france.question: What famous painting is in the Louvre Museum?
france.explanation: The Mona Lisa by Leonardo da Vinci is the Louvre's most famous painting. The museum has over 380,000 artworks!

france.question: Where was Napoleon finally defeated?
france.explanation: Napoleon was defeated at the Battle of Waterloo in 1815 (in modern-day Belgium). This ended his rule over France and much of Europe.

mexico.question: Which ancient civilization built pyramids in Mexico?
mexico.explanation: The Maya built incredible pyramids in Mexico and Central America. They also created an advanced calendar and writing system!

mexico.question: What was the Aztec capital built on?
mexico.explanation: Tenochtitlan was built on an island in a lake! The Aztecs created artificial islands and bridges. Mexico City is built right on top of it.

mexico.question: Which food did Mexico introduce to the world?
mexico.explanation: Mexico gave the world chocolate! The Aztecs made a bitter chocolate drink called 'xocolatl.' They also gave us tomatoes and chili peppers.

mexico.question: What is the Day of the Dead?
options: A celebration to remember loved ones
A day when no one goes outside
mexico.explanation: Día de los Muertos is a beautiful celebration where families remember loved ones who have passed away with flowers, food, and music.

mexico.question: Why is Mexico City sinking?
options: It was built on a lake
mexico.explanation: Mexico City sinks about 20 inches per year because it was built on the soft lakebed where the Aztec capital once stood!

australia.question: How old is Aboriginal Australian culture?
australia.explanation: Aboriginal Australians have the oldest continuous culture on Earth — over 65,000 years! That's older than any other civilization.

australia.question: What was Australia originally used for by the British?
australia.explanation: Britain sent prisoners to Australia starting in 1788. About 160,000 convicts were transported there over 80 years!

australia.question: How many kangaroos live in Australia?
australia.explanation: Australia has about 50 million kangaroos — that's roughly twice the number of people living there!

australia.question: What makes Australia unique among countries?
options: It's both a country and a continent
australia.explanation: Australia is the only place in the world that is both a country and a continent at the same time!

australia.question: What can you see from space off Australia's coast?
australia.explanation: The Great Barrier Reef is so enormous — over 1,400 miles long — that astronauts can see it from space!
